sanskrit:
अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता।
सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी॥
transliteration:
adharmaṃ dharmam iti yā manyate tamasāvṛtā
sarvārthān viparītāṃś ca buddhiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī
literal_meaning: That intellect which, shrouded by tamas, deems adharma as dharma and perceives all things in reverse, O Pārtha, is tāmasic.
contextual_meaning: When the discriminating mind is hijacked by the tamasic field, moral inversion occurs: harm is experienced as helpful, truth as threatening, and self-destructive choices feel virtuous.
psychological_state: Delusional conviction masquerading as clarity; the ego fortifies itself against corrective feedback by pathologizing the good and sanctifying the harmful.
trigger_scenario: A leader who frames cruelty as 'tough love' and compassion as 'weakness,' rallying followers to persecute the very groups that moral traditions protect.
anchor_text: When darkness distorts your compass, pause and seek light before acting.
